guide_review_response.summary:
EA-1's STOP at Item 3.2.v2 surfaced a governance/ count mismatch (7 spec vs 17 actual). Initial verdict authored a per-file selective-copy disposition for that one finding. LA's directive to perform a deeper "mature not minimal" walkthrough across the files surfaced SIX additional compound findings, all of which interact with the same platform-separation correctness boundary. This verdict consolidates the seven findings into a single comprehensive disposition.
SEVEN COMPOUND FINDINGS:
F1 -- governance/ misclassification (17 files = 3 fleet + 12 BlarAI-runtime + 2 meta). INFRA_DELTA_v2.md §1.2 misclassified 4 of 7 (deployment-verification, observability, rule-engine, README are BlarAI-runtime, not platform-fleet). Sprint 9 EA-1..5 expanded the directory to 17 files post-INFRA_DELTA authoring; all 10 additions are BlarAI-runtime. Bulk-copy would create a category error AND set up Stage 5.5.v2 recursive-delete to permanently destroy 14 BlarAI-side files (12 runtime + README + STYLE).
F2 -- docs/runbooks/ Stage 3.2 + Stage 5.5 pattern gap (12 fleet runbooks). BlarAI/docs/runbooks/ contains 12 fleet/LA/MCP/SDO operational runbooks (AUTONOMOUS_FLEET_OPERATIONS, COMMIT_HANDSHAKE_PATTERN, LA_CAR_WORKFLOW_HOWTO, LA_FLEET_REPORTS_HOWTO, LA_OPERATIONS_INDEX, LA_REBOOT_CHECKLIST, LA_SPRINT_DEBRIEF/DISCOVERY/KICKOFF_HOWTO, MCP_CONFIG_SYNC, MCP_REFRESH_DRILL, SDO_PROMPT_DISCIPLINE_CHECKLIST). All 12 are unambiguously platform-fleet. Stage 3.2 v1 patterns (CLAUDE_*, CO_LEAD_*, DEC*, DOMAIN*, CONTINUATION_PROMPT.md) target only top-level docs/*.{md,xml} and don't recurse into subdirs. Stage 3.2.v2 v2_updates also doesn't address runbooks/. Stage 5.5 v1 + 5.5.v2 delete patterns also miss runbooks/. fleet-hygiene.md cross-references 4 of these runbooks (LA_OPERATIONS_INDEX, AUTONOMOUS_FLEET_OPERATIONS, COMMIT_HANDSHAKE_PATTERN, LA_REBOOT_CHECKLIST); without runbooks/ at devplatform, fleet-hygiene's cross-refs break post-cutover.
F3 -- Top-level docs/ orphan fleet files Stage 3.2 patterns missed (2 files). (a) F3_BASH_FORK_ERROR_RUNBOOK.md (471 lines) is a Friction-point F-3 runbook for Claude Code Bash fork errors on Windows -- pure platform/fleet domain (Claude Code workflow). Located at top-level BlarAI/docs/ instead of in runbooks/ (architectural inconsistency). Stage 3.2 patterns don't match (no prefix CLAUDE_/CO_LEAD_/DEC/DOMAIN/CONTINUATION_PROMPT). Stage 5.5 v1 patterns also don't match. Result: orphaned in BlarAI post-cutover; fleet ops at devplatform lose access to a critical Bash-fork-recovery runbook. (b) domain8_spike_findings.md is the Domain 8 (autonomy budget) Scheduler Feasibility Spike Findings document -- pure platform/fleet domain. Lowercase doesn't match Stage 3.2's DOMAIN*.xml (uppercase) pattern. Referenced by 5 devplatform docs at devplatform/docs/ via Stage 3.2 prior copy (CLAUDE_DESKTOP_CONFIGURATION_AGENT_DOMAIN9_INIT.xml + DOMAIN8_DEC11_BUDGET_PROPOSAL_v2.xml + v3.xml + DOMAIN8_DEC11_BUDGET_REVIEW_v3.xml + DOMAIN8_PROGRESS_REVIEW_v1.xml). These 5 already at devplatform have BROKEN cross-references to domain8_spike_findings.md until the file is also at devplatform.
F4 -- Stage 3.3 path drift (claude_cowork + claude_projects). Stage 3.3 IF condition checks top-level BlarAI/claude_cowork/ + BlarAI/claude_projects/ -- both directories EMPTY on disk at 2026-04-27. Actual content lives at BlarAI/docs/claude_cowork/ (1 file: 01_EA_COWORK_INSTRUCTIONS.md) and BlarAI/docs/claude_projects/ (6 files: 01-04 instructions + CUSTOM_INSTRUCTIONS_UI_PASTE.md + README.md). Stage 3.3 literal execution silently skips both (no .Count -gt 0 triggered) -- claude_cowork/projects content orphaned in BlarAI post-cutover. EA-1 will hit this gap at the very next Item (3.3) immediately after 3.2.v2 closure.
F5 -- Stage 5.3 path drift (top-level sprints + scheduled empty no-ops; docs/reports/ uncovered). Stage 5.3 moves block targets BlarAI/sprints/ + BlarAI/scheduled/ (top-level dirs; both EMPTY on disk → no-op moves) plus BlarAI/docs/active_tasks.yaml + BlarAI/docs/sprints/ (correct). Doesn't address BlarAI/docs/reports/ (8 SDO firing exit reports + task_28/ subdir with sprint-hardening completion reports + corrective_actions/.gitkeep). reports/ contains per-project fleet runtime artifacts that should move to BlarAI/.platform/docs_reports/ per Master Plan §A1 ("per-project runtime artifacts ... live under BlarAI/.platform/"). Also doesn't address BlarAI/docs/scheduled/ea_queue/ (per-sprint EA archives) which per INFRA_DELTA_v2.md §3.4 should stay in BlarAI but the §3.4 path itself is wrong (claims docs/ea_queue/ but actual is docs/scheduled/ea_queue/).
F6 -- INFRA_DELTA_v2.md multiple drifts. §1.2 misclassification (F1 above). §1.6 line 121 + §1.7 line 125 + line 134 + line 337 escalation_watchdog HYPHEN typo (per prior 3.1.v2 verdict's g7-ea1_n11 + Ticket E). §3.4 ea_queue path drift (claims docs/ea_queue/; actual docs/scheduled/ea_queue/). Also missing entirely: §1.X entries for docs/runbooks/ (12 files; F2 above), F3_BASH_FORK_ERROR_RUNBOOK.md, domain8_spike_findings.md.
F7 -- Cross-reference graph dependencies (mapped during disk verification). STYLE.md is referenced by 8 BlarAI-runtime governance docs + fleet-hygiene.md + README = 10 referencing files. fleet-hygiene.md additionally cross-references observability.md + deployment-verification.md (BlarAI runtime; would be broken at devplatform post-copy) + 4 runbooks (LA_OPS_INDEX, AUTONOMOUS_FLEET_OPS, COMMIT_HANDSHAKE_PATTERN, LA_REBOOT_CHECKLIST; resolves correctly post-runbooks-copy) + 2 parent docs/ refs (CLAUDE_AUTONOMOUS_FLEET_OPS_D8 + DOMAIN8_DEC11_BUDGET_PROPOSAL_v3; already at devplatform). parallel-sprints.md cross-refs ledger paths (POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER + ledger/ -- BlarAI permanent; soft refs broken at devplatform). Implication: STYLE.md must exist at BOTH repos post-cutover; fleet-hygiene's runtime-doc refs need cleanup at devplatform; parallel-sprints ledger refs need cleanup at devplatform.
DISPOSITION -- Option X-COMPREHENSIVE:
For Stage 3.2.v2 (THIS VERDICT, immediate execution by EA-1):
Step 1 -- governance/ selective copy (4 files: 3 fleet doctrine + STYLE): New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance -Force | Out-Null Copy-Item C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\fleet-hygiene.md C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance\ Copy-Item C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\merge-policy.md C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance\ Copy-Item C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance\ Copy-Item C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\STYLE.md C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance\ Do NOT copy: README.md or any of the 12 BlarAI-runtime governance docs. STYLE.md COPIED (not just-stays-in-BlarAI) to support fleet-hygiene.md:24 `[STYLE.md](STYLE.md)` cross-reference at devplatform; STYLE also stays in BlarAI for the 8+1+1 referrers there. Both copies start byte-identical.
Step 2 -- wake_templates/ recursive copy (6 files): New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\scheduled\wake_templates -Force | Out-Null Copy-Item -Recurse C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\scheduled\wake_templates\*.md C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\scheduled\wake_templates\
Step 3 -- runbooks/ recursive copy (12 files; CLOSES F2 pattern gap): Copy-Item -Recurse C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\runbooks\*.md C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\runbooks\ (devplatform/docs/runbooks/ already exists from Stage 1 scaffold.)
Step 4 -- top-level orphan fleet files (2 files; CLOSES F3 pattern gap): Copy-Item C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\F3_BASH_FORK_ERROR_RUNBOOK.md C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\runbooks\ Copy-Item C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\domain8_spike_findings.md C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\ F3 lands in runbooks/ (its architecturally-correct location, despite originally being authored at top-level docs/) -- this consolidates runbook content at a single path post-cutover. domain8_spike_findings lands at top-level devplatform/docs/ (same source location; resolves the 5 broken cross-refs from devplatform DOMAIN8 docs).
Step 5 -- verification: Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance\ -> expect 4 files Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\runbooks\ -> expect 13 files (12 runbooks + F3 reclassified) Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\scheduled\wake_templates\*.md -> expect 6 files Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\domain8_spike_findings.md -> True Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\ -> expect 17 files (UNCHANGED) Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\runbooks\ -> expect 12 files (UNCHANGED) Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\scheduled\wake_templates\ -> expect 6 files (UNCHANGED) Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\F3_BASH_FORK_ERROR_RUNBOOK.md -> True (UNCHANGED at Stage 3) Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\domain8_spike_findings.md -> True (UNCHANGED at Stage 3) BlarAI source untouched per Stage 3 invariant; Stage 5 EA selectively deletes per cumulative-ack inheritance from Stage 3.10 STATUS append (g7-ea1_n12).
For Stage 3.3 (BOUND IN ADVANCE; EA-1 executes after Stage 3.2.v2 verification per F4):
EA-1 MUST deviate from the literal Stage 3.3 IF condition (which checks empty top-level BlarAI/claude_cowork/ + BlarAI/claude_projects/) and instead source from the actual content paths at BlarAI/docs/claude_cowork/ + BlarAI/docs/claude_projects/. Destination at devplatform: mirror the source location (devplatform/docs/claude_cowork/ + devplatform/docs/claude_projects/).
Step 6 -- Stage 3.3 expanded execution (per F4 path-drift correction): New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\claude_cowork -Force | Out-Null New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\claude_projects -Force | Out-Null Copy-Item -Recurse C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\claude_cowork\* C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\claude_cowork\ Copy-Item -Recurse C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\claude_projects\* C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\claude_projects\ Step 7 -- Stage 3.3 verification: Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\claude_cowork\ -> expect 1 file (01_EA_COWORK_INSTRUCTIONS.md) Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\claude_projects\ -> expect 6 files (4 instructions + CUSTOM + README) Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\claude_cowork\01_EA_COWORK_INSTRUCTIONS.md -> True (UNCHANGED at Stage 3) Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\claude_projects\README.md -> True (UNCHANGED at Stage 3)
For Stage 5 (DELIBERATELY MINIMAL forward-binding via g7-ea1_n12; Stage 5 EA fresh-eyes verification expected on the rest):
The Guide-#7 walkthrough at 2026-04-27 surfaced 7 compound findings across 3 iterations -- a pattern of escalating discovery indicating that further drifts may remain unsurfaced. Per LA + Guide-#7 confidence-calibration discussion at 2026-04-27, Stage 5 amendments are scoped to the MINIMUM required for data-loss prevention. Other Stage 5 implications are noted as "Stage 5 EA STOP-and-verify expected" -- relying on Stage 5 EA's independent fresh-eyes verification at iter-1 gate as the defense-in-depth second check. Class A (data-loss prevention) is bound; Class B (duplication-avoidance) is NOT bound (recoverable; Stage 5 EA STOP-cycle disposes correctly with fresh disk-state at Stage 5 timing).
BINDING AMENDMENT (Class A; data-loss prevention; DO NOT skip): - 5.5.v2 governance/: SELECTIVE delete of only the 3 platform-fleet doctrine files (fleet-hygiene.md, merge-policy.md, parallel-sprints.md) from BlarAI/docs/governance/. PRESERVE 14 BlarAI files (12 runtime + README + STYLE). Without this binding, literal Stage 5.5.v2 executes `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance` and IRREVERSIBLY DESTROYS 14 files of BlarAI runtime governance documentation. - 5.5.v2 wake_templates/: full recursive delete (literal Stage 5.5.v2 behavior correct; all 6 are pure fleet-domain; not technically a Guide-#7 amendment, just confirmation that literal behavior is correct).
NOTED-FOR-STAGE-5-EA (Class B duplication-avoidance + Class C functional-regression; NOT binding amendments; Stage 5 EA fresh-eyes STOP-and-verify expected): Class B (duplication-avoidance): - F-N1: BlarAI/docs/runbooks/ (12 fleet runbooks copied to devplatform at Stage 3.2.v2 Step 3; literal Stage 5.5 patterns don't delete; if Stage 5 EA observes duplication it should STOP and request disposition). - F-N2: BlarAI/docs/F3_BASH_FORK_ERROR_RUNBOOK.md (copied at Stage 3.2.v2 Step 4; same pattern as F-N1). - F-N3: BlarAI/docs/domain8_spike_findings.md (copied at Stage 3.2.v2 Step 4; same). - F-N4: BlarAI/docs/claude_cowork/ + BlarAI/docs/claude_projects/ (copied at Stage 3.3 Step 6; same). - F-N5: BlarAI/docs/reports/ (8+ fleet runtime artifacts; per Master Plan §A1 per-project pattern, candidate for move to BlarAI/.platform/docs_reports/; Stage 5.3 doesn't currently move). - F-N6: BlarAI/docs/scheduled/ea_queue/ (per INFRA_DELTA §3.4 STAYS in BlarAI but path-drift between INFRA_DELTA's claim of docs/ea_queue/ vs actual docs/scheduled/ea_queue/; Stage 5 EA disposition). - F-N7: Top-level BlarAI/sprints/ + BlarAI/scheduled/ EMPTY (literal Stage 5.3 moves are no-ops; not data-loss; document at Stage 5 close). Class C (functional regression; surfaced in Guide-#7 4th-iteration review at 2026-04-27 -- AFTER prior verdict revisions; further validates the inventory-audit requirement): - F-N8: BlarAI/.vscode/tasks.json -- Defines "Run Vikunja Bridge Daemon" task invoking `python -m tools.vikunja_mcp.bridge --verbose`. Post Stage 5.1 deletion of BlarAI/tools/vikunja_mcp/, this VS Code task fails with ModuleNotFoundError when invoked from BlarAI workspace. Stage 4.5 + Stage 5 patterns don't update tasks.json. Disposition options for Stage 5 EA: (a) edit BlarAI/.vscode/tasks.json to remove the Vikunja Bridge task at Stage 5; (b) edit to repoint to absolute devplatform path; (c) add a Stage 6.7.5 ticket only (low-priority post-procedure; LA fixes tasks.json manually if needed). Functional break is annoyance-level; not data-loss + not blocking; Stage 5 EA fresh-eyes disposition appropriate. - F-N9: devplatform/.vscode/ scope at Stage 3.6+ -- Stage 3.6 specifies only `.vscode/mcp.json` creation at devplatform. devplatform/.vscode/ currently empty; post-Stage-3.6 will have only mcp.json. BlarAI's .vscode/ also has tasks.json (Vikunja Bridge task is fleet-relevant; should mirror to devplatform), launch.json (BlarAI-runtime; correctly stays in BlarAI), settings.json (Python interpreter + venv config; devplatform needs its own equivalent for dev ergonomics). Stage 3.6 EA may want to expand scope to include devplatform-specific tasks.json + venv settings -- defer to Stage 3.6 EA fresh-eyes disposition. Not Stage 3.2.v2 scope. - F-N10: BlarAI/pyproject.toml line 13-21 testpaths includes `tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests` (line 18). Stage 5.6's verbatim remove pattern says "remove anything referencing tools/vikunja_mcp, tools/fleet_*, tools/autonomy_budget" -- gate_stale_cleaner is NOT in this pattern list. Stage 5 EA following Stage 5.6 literally would leave tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests in pyproject.toml, causing pytest collection error post-Stage-5.1 deletion of tools/gate_stale_cleaner/. Surfaced in Guide-#7 5th-iteration review at 2026-04-27; further validates the escalating-discovery pattern + the inventory-audit requirement. Class C functional regression (recoverable; pytest collection failure breaks `pytest` invocation without explicit dirs but Stage 5.8 verify uses explicit `pytest shared/ services/` which bypasses testpaths so Stage 5.8 itself doesn't fail). Stage 5 EA disposition.
Class D (test-fixture functional failure post-cutover; surfaced in Guide-#7 5th-iteration review at 2026-04-27): - F-N11: BlarAI/tools/tests/ contains 6 test files (test_project_context.py + test_v_matrix_v4_cli_flag.py + test_v_matrix_v5_ps1_blarairoot.py + test_v_matrix_v6_mcp_project_id.py + test_v_matrix_v7_cross_project_byte_identity.py + test_vikunja_client_scope.py) that import directly from `tools._project_context`, `tools._vikunja_client`, `tools.vikunja_mcp`. Stage 5.1 DELETES all three import targets. Stage 3.1 did NOT copy tools/tests/ to devplatform (devplatform/tools/tests/ doesn't exist on disk per 2026-04-27 verification). Stage 5.1's keep_in_blarai clause says "tools/tests/ -- keep BlarAI-specific tests; delete only platform tool tests" -- but tools/tests/ contents at 2026-04-27 are PLATFORM/FLEET tests (V matrix + chokepoint scope tests; testing the chokepoint contract that lives at tools/_vikunja_client.py + tools/_project_context.py). Spec inconsistency between Stage 3.1 (intended selective-copy per do_not_copy clause; actual command doesn't implement) + Stage 5.1 (vague keep_in_blarai clause assuming tools/tests are BlarAI-specific) + reality (tools/tests are fleet tests). Disposition: tools/tests/ should have been selectively copied to devplatform/tools/tests/ at Stage 3.1 (chokepoint test code travels with chokepoint implementation). Stage 5.1 should then delete BlarAI/tools/tests/. Stage 5 EA fresh-eyes verification + disposition; NOT bound here per LA scope-down directive. Class D failure recoverable (chokepoint testing capability lost at devplatform until tests copied; BlarAI pytest tools/tests fails post-Stage-5.1 until tests deleted from BlarAI).
RATIONALE for minimum-binding scope: Each iteration of Guide-#7 walkthrough has surfaced findings of a DIFFERENT class: Iter 1: per-file domain misclassification (governance/) Iter 2: subdir pattern gap (runbooks/) Iter 3: top-level orphans + path drifts (F3, domain8_spike, claude_cowork/projects, reports, INFRA_DELTA §3.4, cross-ref graph) Iter 4: hidden-config functional regression (.vscode/tasks.json) + workspace-config scope (.vscode/ broader than mcp.json) The findings are NOT converging in kind. Each iteration finds a NEW CLASS, not refinements of prior classes. There is no defensible ground for confidence that iter 4 caught everything. Binding more amendments based on a 4th-iteration-still- finding-new-classes analysis trades Stage-5-EA fresh-verification for premature Guide-confidence.
Class A (data-loss prevention) warrants binding because the cost of NOT binding is IRREVERSIBLE (data destruction). Class B (duplication-avoidance) does NOT warrant binding because the cost of NOT binding is RECOVERABLE (duplicate files; Stage 5 EA STOP-cycle disposes correctly). Class C (functional regression) does NOT warrant binding because the cost of NOT binding is RECOVERABLE (workspace task fails; annoyance-level; LA can edit tasks.json manually post-cutover). Stage 5 timing (days/weeks newer than 2026-04-27 snapshot) means Stage 5 EA's fresh-eyes verification is on more-current disk state than my Guide-#7 snapshot.
The IN-PROCEDURE amendment mechanism is **cumulative-ack inheritance via STATUS.md**: g7-ea1_n12 lands at Stage 3.10 STATUS append → future Guide instance authoring Stage 5 EA initialization pointer MUST embed g7-ea1_n12 verbatim → Stage 5 EA's iter-1 gate restates + acknowledges before executing 5.3 + 5.5 + 5.5.v2 → Stage 5 EA executes selective behavior. Stage 6.7.5 hardening tickets are the COMPLEMENTARY post-procedure mechanism for amending the locked artifacts themselves; they are NOT the in-procedure amendment vehicle (Stage 5 happens BEFORE Stage 6.7.5; tickets cannot retroactively direct Stage 5 EA behavior). This pattern matches the Stage-2-precedent established by g6-ea6_n8 V4 disposition + g7-ea1_n10 STATUS-staleness-correction + g7-ea1_n11 escalation_watchdog typo.
For Stage 6.7.5 (POST-procedure XML/doc cleanup; FOUR REQUIRED tickets per LA broadened mature-not-minimal directive at 2026-04-27):
TICKET A (REQUIRED; HIGH): Consolidated locked-artifact amendments for governance/ + runbooks/ + top-level orphans + claude_cowork/projects + reports/ + ea_queue path drifts (spans INFRA_DELTA_v2.md §1.2/§1.6/§1.7/§3.4 + missing §1.X entries + 04_STAGE3_COPY_TOOLS.xml §3.2 + §3.2.v2 + §3.3 + 06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml §5.3 + §5.5 + §5.5.v2). Architectural-correctness across 3 locked artifacts; data-loss prevention if Stage 5 EA inheritance breaks; affects future stage-procedure design and any re-execution of platform-separation logic.
TICKET B (REQUIRED; LOW): BlarAI README.md edit post-Stage-5 to remove orphan Fleet Hygiene cluster section (lines 152-168) + audience-matrix row (line 188) referring to fleet-hygiene.md which moved to devplatform.
TICKET C (REQUIRED; LOW): devplatform fleet-hygiene.md broken peer-governance refs cleanup -- observability.md + deployment-verification.md cross-refs at lines 30-31 need rewrite to cross-repo paths OR removal as soft peer-governance bullets. STYLE.md Sprint-9 scope-claim at top of devplatform STYLE.md copy may benefit from edit (LOW priority subsumed in same ticket).
TICKET D (REQUIRED; LOW): devplatform parallel-sprints.md broken ledger soft ref cleanup at line 60 (POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER + docs/ledger/ refs).
PROCEED authorization granted for Stage 3.2.v2 expanded execution per Steps 1-5 + Stage 3.3 corrected execution per Steps 6-7 (immediately after 3.2.v2 verification), then continue to Item 3.4. Stage 3 invariants preserved through both items: BlarAI source untouched (Stage 3 is COPY-ONLY); single substantive devplatform commit at 3.9 folds all copies; fleet stays paused; stash byte-identical; no merge to main.

AUDIT 1 -- STAGE 4 EA iter-1 gate (PRIMARY; pre-cutover; CRITICAL): Stage 4 is the destructive cutover (Vikunja DB migration, MCP repointing, scheduled tasks rewrite, .lnk shortcut update, bridge daemon repoint). Auditing AFTER Stage 4 is too late for Class A risks (data destruction during cutover). The Stage 4 EA's iter-1 gate MUST perform the comprehensive inventory audit BEFORE any Stage 4 substantive work. Required activities:
(1) Disk inventory of EVERY file in BlarAI/ + devplatform/ (top-level + all subdirs recursively for docs/, tools/, .vscode/, .github/, .platform/), with per-file domain classification (BlarAI-runtime vs platform-fleet vs meta) by reading each unfamiliar file's first 25 lines. (3) INFRA_DELTA_v2.md §1-§3 re-validation: every claim cross-checked against current disk state; new drifts surfaced. (4) Cross-stage spec audit: review 04_STAGE3_COPY_TOOLS.xml + 05_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml + 06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml + 07_STAGE6_HARDENING.xml for pattern-vs-disk drifts the Guide-#7 walkthrough at 2026-04-27 may have missed. (5) Hidden-config audit: BlarAI/.vscode/{mcp,tasks,launch,settings}.json + BlarAI/.github/copilot-instructions.md + BlarAI/.github/prompts/* + BlarAI/pyproject.toml + BlarAI/.gitignore + BlarAI/sitecustomize.py + BlarAI/launch_blarai.bat + BlarAI/AGENTS.md + BlarAI/CLAUDE.md + devplatform equivalents -- audit each for fleet-path references that need update at cutover and post-cutover. (10) For every drift surfaced: classify as Class A (data-loss; bind immediately in Stage 4 init pointer amendment) vs Class B (duplication; defer to Stage 5 EA disposition) vs Class C (functional regression; defer; LA option to fix manually) vs Class D (test-fixture; defer to Stage 5 cleanup) vs new class.
Audit overhead estimate: 60-90 minutes Stage 4 EA + Guide review time. Cost of a Class A miss at Stage 4 is irreversible data destruction; audit cost is small relative to that risk.
AUDIT 2 -- STAGE 5 EA iter-1 gate (SECONDARY; pre-cleanup; reaffirmation): Stage 5 EA's iter-1 gate re-runs the same audit with a Stage-4-post-cutover disk snapshot. Required activities are the same as AUDIT 1; intent is to catch any new drifts that emerged between Stage 4 cutover and Stage 5 cleanup (typically days/weeks elapsed). Stage 5 EA additionally validates AUDIT 1's classifications (Class B/C/D items that were deferred at Stage 4 may need disposition at Stage 5).
Pattern recognition: the LA + Guide-#7 calibration at 2026-04-27 explicitly acknowledges the 5-iteration escalating-discovery pattern and the architectural observation that "the platform-separation procedure has accumulated more drift than the procedure was designed to handle gracefully" (INFRA_DELTA_v2.md authored 2026-04-24 same day Sprint 9 wrapped; ~4+ days post-authoring repo evolution to absorb). The two-audit defense-in-depth is the operational fix.
